new_classes.label_de: Perlwein in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (Unterposition 2204 29 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10, that is either (a) put up in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or (b) otherwise packaged and having an excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar, measured at 20 °C, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes that is NOT sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10, (2) semi-sparkling pressure quality (≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar CO₂ excess pressure at 20 °C) via the existing SemiSparklingPressureQuality class and numeric range restrictions on excessPressureBar, (3) container volume > 2 L via LargeContainer. Additionally, the mushroom-stopper bottle presentation is covered implicitly by SemiSparklingPressureQuality (which in the running TBox already models this presentation criterion for semi-sparkling wine). A small gap (~15%) remains: the mushroom-stopper bottle variant (regardless of pressure) is a separate presentation criterion mentioned in the legal text that is not fully modelled as a distinct named quality; MushroomStopperBottleQuality exists in the TBox but is not explicitly linked in a separate restriction branch here. Since both the mushroom-stopper case and the CO₂ pressure case are disjunctive inclusion criteria, full coverage would require an owl:unionOf restriction, which is not supported by the propose_axioms schema. The pressure-based restriction is the primary mechanical discriminator and is fully covered.
